Temple Name: Sri Varaha Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy Temple (Simhachalam)
Address: Q782+G6V, Simhachalam Road, Near Gopalpattam Police Station, Simhachalam
Main Deity: Sri Varaha Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy (Lord Vishnu as Narasimha)
Speciality: Most famous temple in Vizag; 11th-century hilltop temple on Simhachalam Hill; unique Chandanotsavam where deity is covered with sandalwood; blend of Kalinga, Chalukya & Chola architecture
Best Time to Visit: Chandanotsavam (Akshaya Tritiya), early morning, October to March
Special Darshan / Archana Fee: ₹100 special darshan; ₹300 VIP darshan; Sevas from ₹50
Entry Details: Free entry; paid special darshan tickets at counter and online; Rs 300 tickets near entrance, Rs 100 tickets deeper in queue
Expected Budget (per person / family): ₹300 - ₹1500 per person | ₹1000 - ₹5000 for family | ₹2000+ during Chandanotsavam
Prasadam Available: Available – laddu prasadam, anna prasadam in annadanam hall
Festivals Celebrated: Chandanotsavam, Kalyanotsavam, Brahmotsavam, Vaikunta Ekadasi, Navaratri
Dress Code: Traditional wear mandatory; dhoti/saree preferred; no western wear inside
Parking: Large free parking at hilltop; shuttle bus from foothill available
Crowd Level: High always; Very crowded on weekends and during Chandanotsavam
Nearby Attractions: Kailasagiri (10 km), Rushikonda Beach (12 km), Araku Valley (100 km)

Temple Name: ISKCON Temple Visakhapatnam (Sri Sri Radha Damodar Temple)
Address: Hare Krishna Land, Dr NTR Beach Road, Sagar Nagar, Rushikonda
Main Deity: Sri Sri Radha Damodar (Lord Krishna and Radha)
Speciality: ISKCON temple on scenic beach road; grand complex with magnificent architecture; regular Hare Krishna kirtans, Bhagavatam classes, prasadam distribution
Best Time to Visit: Janmashtami, Radhashtami, Gaura Purnima, early morning aarti
Entry Details: Free entry; prasadam hall; guest rooms available on advance booking
Prasadam Available: Available daily – full meals (anna prasadam); Govinda's restaurant on premises
Festivals Celebrated: Janmashtami, Radhashtami, Gaura Purnima, Ratha Yatra, Diwali
Dress Code: Traditional/modest attire; no shorts or sleeveless inside
Photography Allowed: Allowed outside; restricted inside main temple
Nearby Attractions: Rushikonda Beach (1 km), Kailasagiri (5 km), Indira Gandhi Zoo (4 km)

Temple Name: Sri Nookambica Ammavari Temple (Nookalamma Temple), Anakapalle
Main Deity: Goddess Nookambika Ammavaru (Sri Anagha Devi – Shakti form)
Speciality: Ancient temple built in 1450 AD; one of the nine Shakti forms; biggest Nookalamma Jathara celebrated before Ugadi draws thousands from Andhra Pradesh, Telangana and Odisha
Best Time to Visit: Nookalamma Jathara (March/April), Tuesdays, Fridays
Entry Details: Free entry; large jathara fair during festival season
Festivals Celebrated: Nookalamma Jathara, Ugadi, Navaratri, Diwali, Makara Sankranti
Dress Code: Traditional wear preferred
Parking: Ample parking during Jathara; limited otherwise
Crowd Level: Very crowded during Jathara; Medium otherwise
Nearby Attractions: Anakapalle town, Simhachalam (35 km), Devipuram (15 km)

Main Deity: Goddess Sahasrakshi (Devi with thousand eyes) – Sri Chakra Meru
Speciality: Unique 3D Sri Chakra temple; the entire structure is built as a Sri Meru with 2000 goddess idols representing Sri Vidya upasana; one of the rarest temples in India
Best Time to Visit: Navaratri, Devi Jayanti, early morning
Entry Details: Free entry; tantric Sri Vidya worship tradition
Dress Code: Traditional/modest attire
Nearby Attractions: Anakapalle (15 km), Nookambica Temple (15 km), Simhachalam (50 km)

Speciality: 2nd century BC Buddhist monastery complex on hilltop; excavated remains of mahachaityastupa, viharas, and cisterns; panoramic sea views from 130m height
Best Time to Visit: October to February; Buddha Purnima
Entry Details: Archaeological site; ticket at entry gate; guided tours available
Dress Code: Comfortable clothing; no dress code
Crowd Level: Low to Medium; popular on weekends
Nearby Attractions: Bheemunipatnam Beach (5 km), Rushikonda Beach (8 km), INS Kursura Museum (20 km)

Speciality: World's richest and most visited Hindu temple; located at 853m on Seshachalam Hills; receives 80,000–100,000 devotees daily; famous hair donation and Laddu prasadam
Best Time to Visit: Brahmotsavam (Sep–Oct), early morning; avoid festival peaks for shorter queues
Special Darshan / Archana Fee: ₹300 Divya Darshan; ₹1500 Srivari Seva; Sevas from ₹500 to ₹10000+
Entry Details: Free darshan; paid special darshans and sevas bookable at tirumala.org; token/TTD-Q online booking available
Expected Budget (per person / family): ₹2000 - ₹10000 per person | ₹8000 - ₹30000 for family including travel
Prasadam Available: Famous Tirupati Laddu; Anna prasadam; multiple annadanam halls
Dress Code: Traditional wear mandatory; dhoti/saree; no western wear; extra dhoti available for rental
Parking: Large paid parking at Alipiri foothill; shuttle to Tirumala
Crowd Level: Very crowded always; Extremely crowded during Brahmotsavam
Nearby Attractions: Sri Padmavathi Temple Tiruchanur, Kapila Theertham, Chandragiri Fort, Srikalahasti
